user_query:
I'm Dr. Anna Rodriguez. Please set up an automated, daily data pull and summary for poverty headcount data to support my ongoing analysis. Use the indicator code and country ISO3 list in config/pipeline.yaml.

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Data fetch and transform script
- Implement a runnable command-line script at scripts/fetch_poverty.py invoked as:
  python scripts/fetch_poverty.py --config config/pipeline.yaml
- The script must download the World Bank indicator "Poverty headcount ratio at $2.15 a day (2017 PPP)" (indicator code SI.POV.DDAY) for all ISO3 countries listed in the config, using the official World Bank Data API by indicator code (CSV or JSON is fine). Do not use third-party mirrors or require credentials.
- Save the exact unmodified dataset you retrieve to:
  data/raw/YYYY-MM-DD/si_pov_dday_raw.csv
  where YYYY-MM-DD is the local date at runtime.
- Produce a processed summary at:
  data/processed/YYYY-MM-DD/poverty_latest.csv
  with one row per country and the following columns:
  country_code, country_name, latest_year, headcount_ratio, ma3, source, retrieved_at
  • latest_year: the most recent year with a reported value for that country.
  • headcount_ratio: the value for latest_year.
  • ma3: a simple 3-year moving average ending at latest_year if at least three values exist for that country; otherwise leave it blank.
  • source: a short identifier like "World Bank SI.POV.DDAY".
  • retrieved_at: an ISO datetime for when the request was made.
- Append one additional row that aggregates across the selected countries with:
  country_code = "AVG", country_name = "Average of selected countries", latest_year = the maximum latest_year among included countries, and headcount_ratio and ma3 as simple means across the countries for that year (ignore missing values).
- Write or append a plain-text run log with timestamps to logs/poverty_sync.log summarizing start, end, rows fetched, and any countries with missing recent values.
- The script must read indicator_code and countries strictly from config/pipeline.yaml; do not hardcode the country list in code. Handle countries with no recent data by falling back to the most recent available year.

2) Scheduling
- Create a cron definition at scheduler/poverty_sync.cron that runs the above command daily at 06:30 local time, redirecting stdout and stderr to logs/poverty_sync.log (append mode).
- Provide a shell installer at scheduler/install_cron.sh that installs or updates this cron entry for the current user without creating duplicates.

3) Run once now
- After implementing, run the script once so that today’s:
  • data/raw/YYYY-MM-DD/si_pov_dday_raw.csv
  • data/processed/YYYY-MM-DD/poverty_latest.csv
  • logs/poverty_sync.log
  are created in the workspace.

Constraints:
- Construct requests using the official World Bank Data API by the indicator code from the config; do not require API keys or accounts.
- Use only standard command-line tools and Python for implementation.
- Keep paths exactly as specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
